{"step_by_step_thinking": "In electrophoresis, lipoproteins migrate towards the charged end based on their net charge and size. HDL is known to have a net positive charge and moves towards the cathode (negative end) in electrophoresis. VLDL, LDL, and chylomicrons have net negative charges and move towards the anode (positive end). Therefore, HDL is the only lipoprotein that does not move towards the charged end in electrophoresis.", "answer_choice": "C"}
